# North52 Formula Analyzer - Python Dependencies

# ============================================================================
# CORE ANALYSIS - STANDARD LIBRARY ONLY
# ============================================================================
# The core analysis script (analyze_formula.py) uses Python 3.8+ standard library
# modules only for maximum portability.

# Python Standard Library Modules Used:
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# json                   - Reading/writing analysis_metadata.json files
# re                     - Regular expressions for formula code parsing
# pathlib                - Cross-platform file system path operations
# datetime               - Timestamp generation and comparison
# argparse               - Command-line argument parsing for all scripts
# sys                    - System operations and path manipulation
# os                     - Operating system interfaces

# ============================================================================
# DATAVERSE INTEGRATION - OPTIONAL DEPENDENCIES
# ============================================================================
# For fetching formulas directly from Dataverse (fetch_north52_from_dataverse.py)
# Install these only if you want to retrieve formulas from Dataverse API:

# Azure Identity - For authentication to Dataverse
azure-identity>=1.15.0
PowerPlatform-Dataverse-Client>=0.3.0

# MSAL Extensions - For persistent token caching (stores in OS keychain)
msal-extensions>=1.0.0

# Requests - For HTTP API calls
requests>=2.31.0

# Installation for Dataverse Integration:
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# pip install -r requirements.txt
# Or install specific packages:
# pip install azure-identity requests

# To use without Dataverse integration:
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# No installation needed! Just ensure you have Python 3.8 or later:
#   python --version  # Should show 3.8.0 or higher
